shared_groups: Cross-Border Transfer Authorization
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor does not transfer personal data to third countries or international organizations, matching the regulatory non-transfer duty
results.condition_check: Regulation requires no transfer without prior specific or general authorization of the controller; contract states an unconditional non-transfer, which is stricter and therefore compatible
results.modality_check: 'does not transfer' is a prohibitive/restrictive formulation at least as strong as 'shall not'
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing, manner, target, location, or cause constraints; the contract is stricter by prohibiting the transfer outright
results.contract_source_text: The Personal Data is only processed by the Data Processor at the Data Processor’s address. The Data Processor does not transfer the Personal Data to third countries or international organizations.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor shall assist the controller with DPIA-related compliance; contract also includes assisting in preparing DPIAs and prior consultation under GDPR Art. 35/36, which is the same or a broader assistance obligation
results.condition_check: Compatible: contract adds 'if necessary and relevant,' a reasonable qualifier that does not weaken the duty
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' matches the regulatory mandatory modality
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract is at least as specific/broader by covering DPIA preparation and prior consultation under Articles 35 and 36, which falls within or exceeds assistance in ensuring DPIA compliance
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: same processor duty to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract uses employees working on personal data and instruction-based processing, which is substantively the same obligation
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: No differing manner/timing/target/location/cause; contract is at least as strict
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor must ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract says processor may only act and process personal data in accordance with documented controller instructions, which is substantively the same restriction on processing under instruction
results.condition_check: compatible: both are limited to processing on controller instructions; contract is at least as strict by requiring documented instruction
results.modality_check: compatible: 'may only' is restrictive and equal-or-greater force than 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: no additional manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints conflict; contract is not looser
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects; the contract sub-item, read with the parent register clause, requires the register to include a description of the measures the processor has taken or proposes to take to address the breach, including measures to mitigate adverse effects. Same substantive content, with the contract at least as specific.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses 'shall'; the contract sub-item is a mandatory sub-item under a parent 'shall' clause, so force is equal or greater.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; the contract wording is substantively equivalent and not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data matches processor shall process personal data; recipient none
results.condition_check: compatible: contract adds a narrower lawful-processing condition (necessary and required under applicable law) rather than conflicting with documented instructions
results.modality_check: equal force: shall
results.other_constraints_check: compatible/stricter: contract’s three-month post-termination timing is additional and does not weaken the instruction-based manner; parent context expressly treats the processing as in accordance with the Instruction
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must return all personal data to controller; contract requires Data Processor and Sub-Processors to return the Personal Data to the Data Controller, and also delete all the Personal Data with documentation
results.condition_check: compatible: regulation requires action after end of services relating to processing; contract requires return at termination of the agreement, which is equivalent in this context, with an added condition that controller is not already in possession of the data (narrower/stricter, not looser)
results.modality_check: compatible: both are mandatory 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: contract is at termination, which matches or is not weaker than after end of provision of services; added deletion/documentation is stricter and does not weaken the required return
results.contract_source_text: At the termination of this Data Processor Agreement, the Data Processor and its Sub- Processors shall return the Personal Data processed under this Data Processor Agreement to the Data Controller, provided that the Data Controller is not already in possession of the Personal Data. The Data Processor is hereafter obliged to delete all the Personal Data and provide documentation for such deletion to the Data Controller. 11. Contact

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data matches the regulation's processor processing personal data; recipient none in both
results.condition_check: compatible: contract requires processing in accordance with documented instructions from the controller, which matches the regulation's documented instructions condition
results.modality_check: compatible and stricter: contract says 'may only' (restrictive) rather than a bare permission, which is at least as strong as 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: contract's 'with the purpose of delivering the Main Services' is an additional narrowing purpose and does not weaken the required manner of processing on documented instructions
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor informs controller of intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors vs processor notifies controller of identity of a potential sub-processor before engagement; same general sub-processor notice obligation, but contract is narrower and focuses on identity of a potential sub-processor rather than intended changes to addition/replacement
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the context of general written authorization / authorization to use sub-processors
results.modality_check: Equal force: shall
results.other_constraints_check: Contract is stricter on timing and manner (in writing, before agreements and processing), which does not weaken it; however it does not expressly cover 'any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of sub-processors' as such
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures

results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall vs shall; equal force
results.other_constraints_check: Contract is at least as strict: implements appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which meets/exceeds taking all measures required for security of processing
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures; contract uses same actor/action/object and is at least as specific by tying them to the Agreement and GDPR Art. 32
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: Manner is compatible/stricter: contract references measures set out in the Agreement and Applicable Law, including GDPR Article 32, which does not weaken the security-appropriate-to-risk requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the processor's employees are persons authorized to process personal data, and the contract imposes a confidentiality obligation on them, matching the requirement that such persons be committed to confidentiality or under an appropriate statutory obligation
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall matches shall and is not weaker
results.other_constraints_check: with strict confidentiality is at least as protective as confidentiality; no conflicting timing/target/location/cause
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor must return or delete all personal data to controller; contract parent/sub-item requires processor and sub-processors to return personal data to controller at termination and processor to delete all personal data and provide deletion documentation to controller

results.other_constraints_check: return/delete all personal data is equivalent; recipient controller matches; no weaker timing or other limiting constraint

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: the processor shall assist the controller with prior consultation of supervisory authorities before processing; the contract expressly includes prior consultation under GDPR Article 36.

results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states a description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach, which is the same content obligation and is even broader.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: The contract sub-item is a plain descriptive statement within a mandatory parent clause ('shall ... include the following'), so it carries mandatory force at least equal to 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner, timing, target, location, or cause constraints; the contract is at least as specific and broader on content.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include four items: nature of the breach, DPO contact details, consequences, and mitigation measures. The contract sub-item, together with the parent clause, only requires the processor to maintain a register of personal data breaches and to include a description of measures taken or proposed to address the breach, including mitigation measures. That covers only one of the required items and is an internal register obligation, not the notification content as a whole.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side.
results.modality_check: The regulation is mandatory; the contract sub-item is a plain statement within a mandatory parent clause, so force is sufficient for the measure-description item it covers.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, location, or cause issues; however the contract omits several required notification contents and is limited to a register entry.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both concern the processor processing personal data; recipient none

results.modality_check: compatible: 'may only' is restrictive and at least as strong as 'can'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: contract adds a narrower purpose limitation and documented-instruction requirement; these do not weaken the regulation requirement

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the nature of the breach; the contract sub-item, within a mandatory parent clause, requires a description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach (plus optional extra details), which is the same substantive content obligation
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract adds 'if possible' qualifiers for extra details, but still requires the nature description unconditionally

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: the processor owes the controller liability/accountability for sub-processor performance/acts, which is the same substantive obligation
results.condition_check: no condition on either side
results.modality_check: plain statement in a mandatory contract clause is at least as strong as shall
results.other_constraints_check: "in the same way as for its own actions and omissions" is substantively equivalent to full liability for sub-processor obligations
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: regulation requires assistance to the controller in ensuring security of processing; contract requires assistance to the controller only for data-subject-rights requests and related information/documentation
results.condition_check: different condition: request from data subject for exercise of rights vs no condition/security context
results.modality_check: shall vs shall, but modality cannot cure the different obligation
results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner are not relevant to the required security-assistance duty and do not bridge the substantive mismatch
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Controller receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and the correct and legitimate reply to such a request necessitates the Data Processor’s assistance, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller by providing the necessary information and documentation. The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate Article 28 compliance to the controller, while the contract only says the processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided, without specifying that it is the necessary information or that it is provided to the controller
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Shall matches shall.
results.other_constraints_check: Within reasonable time is compatible with an unstated timing requirement and is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response

results.condition_check: compatible: contract triggers on a personal data breach described as a data security breach leading to accidental/unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to personal data; this is substantively the same breach-awareness trigger

results.other_constraints_check: compatible and stricter: immediate notice is at least as strict as without undue delay
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

results.other_constraints_check: manner differs but is not the issue; the substantive object is different (DPIA/prior consultation vs responding to data subject rights requests)

results.modality_check: equal force: shall on both sides
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: reasonable time and providing necessary information/documentation are consistent with, and not looser than, the assistance obligation

The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match the required obligation: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data, while the contract sub-item only imposes confidentiality and a prohibition on processing in conflict with instructions; the parent context adds instruction-compliance, but the sub-item itself is not the same primary obligation
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; contract adds a restriction tied to conflict with the Instruction and controller written agreement, which is not a mismatch for the core duty but does not create the required affirmative processing obligation.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but modality does not cure the missing substantive obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires processing on documented instructions from the controller; the contract only prohibits processing in conflict with the Instruction and treats data as confidential, which is related but not the same affirmative manner requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align only partially: the regulation requires the processor to impose the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors; the contract requires a sub-processor agreement to provide the same data protection obligations as those applicable to the Data Processor, which is substantively the same sub-processor flow-down obligation

The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Same general subject (personal data breach notification content), but the contract only includes the nature of the breach and optional affected-data-subject counts; it omits the DPO contact details, consequences, and mitigation measures required by the regulation.
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; contract adds an optional qualifier ('if possible') on extra details, which does not weaken the omitted required items.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory; parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is a plain descriptive statement within that mandatory register content, so modality is not the issue.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/cause issues. The substantive gap is missing required content items, not ancillary constraints.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: regulation requires ensuring authorized persons are committed to confidentiality or bound by statutory confidentiality, while contract only requires employees processing personal data to process according to instructions
results.condition_check: compatible
results.modality_check: equal force (shall)
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant timing/manner/target differences
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Cross-Border Transfer Authorization
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation prohibits transferring personal data to a third country or international organization without authorization, while the contract imposes confidentiality and a general prohibition on copying/transferring/processing in conflict with instructions, not a cross-border transfer authorization rule
results.condition_check: No matching condition: the regulation is triggered by lack of prior specific or general authorization from the controller; the contract instead refers to conflict with the Instruction and a separate written agreement by the controller
results.modality_check: The contract's 'shall' plus 'may not' is mandatory/restrictive, but it is directed to a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive match on target/location/cause: the regulation's target is transfer to a third country or international organization, which is absent from the contract
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying the supervisory authority of a personal data breach, while the contract requires the processor to give immediate notice to the data controller about a breach
results.condition_check: compatible in that both are triggered by a personal data breach, though the contract defines it more specifically
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: timing is stricter (immediate) and otherwise there is no matching obligation to notify the supervisory authority
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract only requires providing a copy of the register of personal data breaches to the data controller
results.condition_check: regulation has no condition; contract adds a request-in-writing condition, but the main issue is that it is a different duty
results.modality_check: shall vs shall, but modality does not cure the mismatch
results.other_constraints_check: different object and recipient; providing a breach register copy is not the same as assisting notification to the supervisory authority
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract only requires the processor to have and maintain a register of breaches
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'); compatible
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints to compare; but the contract's register obligation is a different duty from breach-notification assistance
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the categories of data subjects, and the parent clause states that the processor processes personal data about the following categories of data subjects; the sub-item 'Customers' is a concrete category of data subjects, matching the required axis
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract sub-item is a plain descriptive statement under a mandatory parent clause, which counts as mandatory and is not weaker than 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints
results.contract_source_text: Customers

shared_groups: Cross-Border Transfer Authorization
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor is restricted from transferring personal data to a third country or international organization; contract prohibits copying/transferring/processing personal data in conflict with the Instruction unless the controller has agreed in writing. This covers transfer of personal data and adds a stricter instruction-based restriction, but it does not specifically mention third countries/international organizations; however the transfer prohibition itself is the same real-world obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: regulation requires no transfer without prior specific or general authorization of the controller; contract allows the act only unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed, which is equivalent or stricter authorization by the controller.
results.modality_check: Compatible: regulatory 'shall not' is matched by contract 'may not', which is equally restrictive.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as strict on timing/authorization (prior written agreement) and does not loosen the transfer restriction; no conflicting manner/location/target limitation beyond the same substantive transfer control.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action align as processor assistance, but object/recipient are about DPIAs and prior consultation for the controller, not communicating a personal data breach to the data subject
results.condition_check: Contract adds 'if necessary and relevant', which is not weaker than an unconditional duty
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: No matching breach-communication-to-data-subject timing/manner/target; instead it concerns Article 35/36 assistance
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action align as processor assisting controller, but the object/recipient are different: contract assists in DPIAs and prior consultation, not in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority

results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: manner/timing do not bridge the substantive mismatch; the contract addresses Article 35/36 assistance, not breach notification to the supervisory authority
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data for the controller is the same underlying obligation
results.condition_check: compatible: contract limits processing to connection with delivery of the Main Services, which is a narrower/expected context and does not conflict with documented instructions

results.other_constraints_check: compatible: 'on behalf of the Data Controller' is consistent with processing on documented instructions from the controller; no contradictory manner/timing/location constraints
results.contract_source_text: In connection with the Data Processor’s delivery of the Main Services to the Data Controller, the Data Processor will process certain categories and types of the Data Controller’s personal data on behalf of the Data Controller.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation

results.condition_check: compatible: the contract adds a request-by-controller condition, which is not inherently weaker for this kind of make-available duty
results.modality_check: compatible: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: not fully aligned on object scope: security-measures documentation is narrower than information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the processor to ensure authorized persons are bound by confidentiality commitments or statutory confidentiality obligations, while the contract only requires treating personal data as strictly confidential and restricting copying/transfer/processing; it does not impose confidentiality commitments on authorized persons

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor processing personal data under law-required circumstances matches processor processing personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by applicable Union/Member State law / Applicable Law necessity and requirement
results.modality_check: Compatible and stronger: contract uses shall, which is at least as strong as can
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract adds a stricter timing limit (up to three months after termination) and does not weaken the no-documented-instructions aspect
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of any intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors, while the contract only states that certain sub-processors have been added and communicated prior to relevant sub-processing; it does not cover intended changes or replacement, and is about a completed list rather than the required notification duty.
results.condition_check: Gap: the regulation is triggered in case of general written authorization; the contract has no corresponding condition.
results.modality_check: Gap: the contract is a plain statement about added sub-processors, not an obligation on the processor to inform the controller.
results.other_constraints_check: Gap: the timing 'prior to the relevant sub-processing' is not equivalent to informing the controller of intended changes under the regulation, and the contract omits the required change-notification substance.
results.contract_source_text: The following Sub-Processors have been added and communicated to the Data Controller prior to the relevant sub-processing:

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Cross-Border Transfer Authorization
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: yes, both concern processor transfers of personal data to third countries or international organizations
results.condition_check: regulation requires prior specific or general authorization of the controller; contract instead requires the transfer be permitted and in accordance with applicable law, which does not mention controller authorization and is not equivalent

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action align (processor shall assist), but the object differs: contract assistance is for responding to data subject rights requests, not for consulting supervisory authorities prior to processing

The Data Processor shall be given reasonable time to assist the Data Controller with such requests in accordance with the Applicable Law.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not fully match: the regulation requires the processor to impose the same Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors, while the contract requires concluding a written sub-processor agreement with sub-processors; however the supporting text adds that the agreement must at minimum provide the same data protection obligations, which substantively covers the same imposition duty.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'); contract is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: Regulation requires doing this by way of contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law; the contract requires a written sub-processor agreement, which is equivalent and satisfies the manner requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align in substance: both concern the processor handling a data subject rights request and supporting the controller's response obligation; however the contract's primary duty is to forward the request and refrain from direct response, not to assist the controller in fulfilling the response obligation
results.condition_check: compatible: both are triggered by a data subject request for exercise of rights
results.modality_check: must is mandatory and at least as strong as shall
results.other_constraints_check: immediately is equivalent/stricter than no timing limit; but the contract omits the substantive assistance component and instead imposes a narrower forwarding duty
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the consequences of the breach, while the contract sub-item (within a mandatory register) requires a description of the nature of the breach and related counts, not the consequences
results.condition_check: No condition on the regulation side; contract adds an optional qualifier ('if possible') but that does not create the missing consequences content
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, so modality is not weaker; however modality cannot cure the missing object
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap for the required 'consequences of the breach' content
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, while the contract only requires the processor to give immediate notice to the controller about a breach
results.condition_check: compatible in part: both are triggered by a personal data breach, but the contract condition is only about notifying the controller and does not cover assisting communication to the data subject
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: timing is stricter (immediate) but the contract lacks the required communication-to-data-subject assistance target
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align only partially: the regulation requires the notification to include the nature of the personal data breach, while the contract sub-item (with parent context) includes a description of the likely and actually occurred consequences of the breach, which is a different Article 33(3) item
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory, and the parent clause is mandatory, but the sub-item content is only a descriptive item and does not supply the required nature-of-breach information
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause issues
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: partially aligned on processor-to-controller security-related assistance, but the contract only requires providing documentation of the processor’s security measures, not assisting the controller in ensuring security of processing
results.condition_check: compatible: both are unconditional in the regulation, while the contract adds a request-by-controller trigger, which is narrower
results.modality_check: shall vs shall: equal force
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant mismatch in timing/manner/target/location/cause, but the substantive duty is different and narrower
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to impose Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors, while the contract only states accountability to the controller for sub-processor acts/omissions
results.condition_check: No condition in either clause
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain accountability statement, not an obligation to impose contractual terms on sub-processors
results.other_constraints_check: The manner/target differ: accountability for acts and omissions is not the same as imposing the same obligations by contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the consequences of the breach; contract requires the register of personal data breaches to be provided in copy to the controller on request
results.condition_check: regulation has no condition; contract is conditional on written request by the controller or data protection agency
results.modality_check: both are shall, but the contract obligation is only to provide the register, not to include breach consequences
results.other_constraints_check: different duty and object; providing a register copy is not the same as including the consequences of the breach in the breach notification
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data, while the contract imposes a guarantee by the data controller about the controller's own processing and lawfulness; this is a different duty and different actor
results.condition_check: no condition in either side
results.modality_check: contract is only a plain statement/guarantee, but modality mismatch is secondary because the obligation itself is different
results.other_constraints_check: manner differs materially: regulation requires processing on documented instructions from the controller; contract instead requires processing in accordance with applicable law/lawfulness by the controller
results.contract_source_text: The Data Controller guarantees that the Personal Data transferred to the Data Processor is processed by the Data Controller in accordance with the Applicable Law, including the legislative requirements re lawfulness of processing.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: both require the processor to process personal data; the contract also states that such processing is considered in accordance with the instruction, matching the documented-instructions manner requirement
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall on contract side matches regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: Contract’s statement that processing is considered in accordance with the Instruction is substantively equivalent to processing on documented instructions from the controller; the extra backup/three-month context does not weaken that obligation
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/sub-processors: the regulation requires the processor to impose the Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act; the contract parent clause requires a written sub-processor agreement with any sub-processors that at minimum provides the same data protection obligations as those applicable to the processor, including the obligations under the DPA. This directly carries the same obligation.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses 'shall'; contract uses 'shall' and is at least as strong.
results.other_constraints_check: Manner matches or exceeds: the regulation requires contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law; the contract requires a written sub-processor agreement, which satisfies the contractual/legal-act requirement and is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: same content obligation, but the contract only points to contact information being provided in the Main Agreement rather than stating the processor identity/contact details here
results.condition_check: no condition on either side
results.modality_check: plain statement is not weaker than shall for a content clause
results.other_constraints_check: no timing/manner/target/location/cause issues; however the clause is a pointer to another document, not the content itself
results.contract_source_text: The contact information for the Data Processor and the Data Controller is provided in the Main Agreement..

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: regulation requires the breach notification to include the DPO's name and contact details, while the contract requires the register of personal data breaches to be provided in copy to the controller
results.condition_check: regulation has no condition; contract adds a request-in-writing condition, but the main mismatch is that it is a different duty
results.modality_check: both are mandatory, but modality is irrelevant because the obligations are different
results.other_constraints_check: no substantive overlap on the required content; the contract is about providing a breach register, not including DPO contact details in a notification
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: regulation requires the processor to ensure any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes only on controller instructions; contract only imposes confidentiality on the processor’s employees, not instruction-limited processing
results.condition_check: No condition in either side
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but the contract obligation is a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing/location/cause; confidentiality is a different protective duty than instruction-only processing
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s employees shall be subject to an obligation of confidentiality that ensures that the employees shall treat all the Personal Data under this Data Processor Agreement with strict confidentiality.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors, while the contract requires the processor to conclude written agreements with sub-processors
results.condition_check: regulation is triggered only in case of general written authorization; the contract has no corresponding notification condition
results.modality_check: both are mandatory ('shall'), but the contract obligation is a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap; 'written' manner on the contract does not address the required informing duty
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the consequences of the breach, while the contract sub-item (with parent context) requires a register entry/description of measures taken or proposed to address the breach and mitigate adverse effects; that is a different content item, not the breach consequences themselves.
results.condition_check: No condition issue; both are unconditional.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, so the sub-item is mandatory as well, but modality does not cure the substantive mismatch.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/target/cause mismatch relevant.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor obligations, while the contract requires ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processors' compliance and a written sub-processor agreement; that is a different duty, not liability
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'); compatible
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs but is not relevant to the liability obligation; the contract's ongoing monitoring is a separate, different obligation rather than an equivalent or stricter form of liability
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: no same obligation; regulation requires the DPA to contain at least one controller's identity and contact details, while the contract merely points to contact information for both processor and controller being provided in the Main Agreement
results.condition_check: no condition on either side
results.modality_check: plain statement is not weaker than shall, but modality is irrelevant because the substantive obligation is different
results.other_constraints_check: no timing/manner/target/location/cause issues
results.contract_source_text: The contact information for the Data Processor and the Data Controller is provided in the Main Agreement..

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the nature of the personal data breach, while the contract only names 'Personal Data Breaches' as a topic/label and does not state the required content.
results.condition_check: No condition issue; the regulation has no condition and the contract adds none.
results.modality_check: The contract is only a plain label/statement, not an obligation to include the required information.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause issues are present.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: the regulation requires the processor, when assessing security, to take into account specific data-security risks; the contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32. This is related to Article 32 security obligations but it is a different duty (implement measures vs. take into account risks when assessing security), so the same real-world obligation is not directly carried.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered when assessing the level of security; the contract has no matching assessment condition.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory ('shall'), but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/location/target overlap that bridges the substantive difference.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor shall provide documentation substantiating compliance information to the controller on request, which is the same Article 28(3)(h) duty to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance
results.condition_check: Compatible: contract adds a written-request trigger, which is a reasonable narrower condition and does not weaken the duty
results.modality_check: Equivalent force: 'shall' matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Substantively equivalent: 'provide documentation substantiating that' is a narrower-sounding but acceptable way to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance; no conflicting timing/manner/location limits
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall after the Data Controller’s written request hereof provide documentation substantiating that:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: regulation requires the notification of a personal data breach to include the measures taken or proposed to mitigate its effects; the contract sub-item (with parent register context) only includes a description of the likely and actually occurred consequences, which is a different content item
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory ('shall'); the parent clause is mandatory and the sub-item is a mandatory included item within that register
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause differences
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object: the regulation requires inclusion of pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data as one possible security measure; the contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which encompasses and exceeds that measure set
results.condition_check: no condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: regulatory 'can' is permissive/illustrative, while contract 'shall' is mandatory and stronger
results.other_constraints_check: no conflicting manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints; contract is broader and stricter
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the DPA to contain the duration of processing (a content clause), while the contract only states the agreement remains in force until termination (a term/duration of the agreement itself), not the processing duration
results.condition_check: compatible but irrelevant; contract condition concerns agreement termination, not processing duration
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap
results.contract_source_text: The Data processor Agreement shall remain in force until the Main Agreement is terminated. 10. Termination

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures, while the contract only requires the processor to provide documentation of its security measures to the controller on request
results.condition_check: compatible in that the contract adds a request condition, but this is not the same obligation
results.modality_check: shall matches shall, but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation
results.other_constraints_check: the regulation’s security-level/appropriateness requirement is not met by a documentation-provision duty; the contract addresses disclosure of documentation rather than implementation of measures
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not describe the same obligation: the regulation imposes controller liability for damage caused by infringing processing, while the contract imposes processor accountability to the controller for sub-processor acts/omissions
results.condition_check: compatible but irrelevant; both are unconditional
results.modality_check: contract is a plain mandatory statement, but it addresses a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: not applicable; the contract's manner/recipient/object differ materially from the regulation's liability-for-damage requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: regulation requires the breach notification itself to include the nature of the personal data breach; contract requires the breach register to be provided in copy to the controller on request
results.condition_check: regulation has no condition; contract is conditional on written request, so not the same obligation
results.modality_check: both are mandatory ('shall'), but modality does not cure the mismatch
results.other_constraints_check: recipient and content differ: providing a copy of the register is not the same as including the nature of the breach in the notification
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires including measures to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services; the contract requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which substantively covers that security obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. No special triggering condition in the regulation; the contract is a general obligation to implement the measures.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall implement' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'can include' in the regulation's measure-list context.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's reference to Article 32 and appropriate technical and organizational measures is substantively aligned with the security measures requirement; no weaker timing, manner, target, location, or cause limitation appears.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: regulation requires the processor to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract concerns the processor continuing to process personal data after termination and backup inclusion, not instruction-limited processing by natural persons under authority
results.condition_check: No substantive match; contract condition is necessity under applicable law, not the access/instruction condition in the regulation
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory, but modality does not cure the mismatch in obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner are unrelated to the regulation's requirement
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to return or delete all personal data to the controller, while the contract only says the processor's authorization to process personal data is annulled at termination; that is a different duty and does not require return/deletion or delivery to the controller
results.condition_check: not compatible: regulation triggers after the end of the provision of services relating to processing, while the contract triggers at termination of the agreement; similar timing, but the substantive obligation is different anyway
results.modality_check: shall is mandatory on both sides, but the contract's mandatory duty is only annulment of authorization, not return/delete
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant matching manner/target/location/cause; the contract omits the required return-or-delete action and controller recipient
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s authorization to process Personal Data on behalf of the Data Controller shall be annulled at the termination of this Data Processor Agreement.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align on processor processing personal data, but the contract clause is about processing in connection with delivery of services and on behalf of the controller, not processing without documented instructions under a legal requirement
results.condition_check: Regulation is limited to processing if required by Union or Member State law; contract condition is service delivery, which is different
results.modality_check: Contract uses mandatory 'will', but it does not grant the same legal-exception permission required by the regulation
results.other_constraints_check: Regulation requires processing without documented instructions; contract does not address absence of documented instructions and instead ties processing to service delivery/on behalf of controller
results.contract_source_text: In connection with the Data Processor’s delivery of the Main Services to the Data Controller, the Data Processor will process certain categories and types of the Data Controller’s personal data on behalf of the Data Controller.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors; contract instead requires monitoring and controlling sub-processors' compliance and providing documentation on request
results.condition_check: regulation applies in case of general written authorization; contract has no corresponding condition
results.modality_check: both are mandatory ('shall'), so modality is compatible, but the substantive duty differs
results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner/target/location/cause do not supply the missing informing duty; ongoing monitoring is a different obligation than notifying intended changes
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the categories of data subjects (a class of persons), while the contract only mentions 'Rights of the data subjects,' which is a different subject and does not state any categories of data subjects.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side.
results.modality_check: The regulation is mandatory, but the contract's plain statement does not supply the required content; modality is not the main issue.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, location, or cause issues.
results.contract_source_text: Rights of the data subjects

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor duty to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions matches processor prohibition on processing personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions
results.condition_check: Compatible: both allow deviation only with controller authorization/instruction; contract is at least as strict
results.modality_check: Compatible: contract's 'may not' is restrictive and satisfies the processor's mandatory 'shall ensure' duty
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'in conflict with the Instruction' and 'only process them on instructions from the controller' are substantively equivalent, with the contract not looser
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Cross-Border Transfer Authorization
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation prohibits transferring personal data to a third country or international organization without authorization, while the contract imposes confidentiality and a general no-processing-in-conflict-with-instruction rule; the parent context mentions transfer only as one prohibited form of conflicting processing, not the specific cross-border transfer authorization duty
results.condition_check: The contract's relevant transfer-related language is conditioned on conflict with the Instruction and written agreement, which is not the same as a prior specific or general authorization for transfer to a third country or international organization.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a prohibition only in a different context (no processing in conflict with the Instruction unless agreed), but it does not impose the specific transfer prohibition required by the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, recipient, or location specifics for cross-border transfer authorization are provided; the confidentiality clause is substantively different.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: regulation requires the processor to ensure that natural persons under its authority with access to personal data process them only on controller instructions; contract only says the processor complies with its obligations under the DPA and the Instruction, which is a general compliance statement and does not specifically impose the personnel-instructions restriction
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract is a plain mandatory statement within a parent 'shall' clause, but it does not impose the specific duty required

results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with its obligations under this Data Processor Agreement and the Instruction; and

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the nature and purpose of the processing, while the contract requires the processor to have and maintain a register of processing activities; this is a different duty and does not state the required processing nature/purpose content
results.condition_check: No condition on either side
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory (shall)
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/target/location/cause mismatch; manner clause is unrelated to the required content
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of processing activities in accordance with GDPR, article 32 (2).

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align substantively: the DPA must state the types of personal data, and the contract clause states the following types of Personal Data in-line; this is the same content obligation, not merely adjacent processing activity

results.modality_check: Contract plain statement within a mandatory clause context is at least as strong as 'shall' for this content requirement
results.other_constraints_check: Manner 'in connection with its delivery of the Main Services' does not weaken or contradict the requirement to contain the types of personal data
